rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed electrochemical C-H/N-H annulation of 8-aminoquinoline benzamides with alkenes to give dihydroisoquinolinone derivatives
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the active Co(III) catalyst from Co(I)/Co(II); cathode reduces protons to H2. The electrode shuttles electrons via the cobalt catalyst rather than directly oxidizing substrate.

key_intermediates.name: Co(I) species released after reductive elimination

electron_flow: Co(II) binds the benzamide N-H, is anodically oxidized to Co(III), then performs BIES C-H metalation; alkene coordinates and migratory insertion gives a 7-membered cobaltacycle that reductively eliminates product and Co(I), which is reoxidized at the anode while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: C-H activation step (BIES-type concerted metalation-deprotonation), supported by KIE = 1.2 and highly unsymmetrical transition state

probe_reagents.name: CD3OD (H/D exchange)
probe_reagents.observation: <5% D incorporation in recovered 1a and in product 3aa, indicating C-H metalation is likely irreversible

probe_reagents.name: [D5]-1a deuterated benzamide
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD = 1.2, suggesting C-H cleavage is involved but with a highly unsymmetrical TS

probe_reagents.name: Competition between 1b (Me) and 1h (CF3) benzamides
probe_reagents.observation: 3ba:3ha = 1.7:1, electron-rich amide reacts faster, consistent with BIES pathway

probe_reagents.name: Three-component coupling with benzoyl chloride 9 + 8-aminoquinoline 10 + styrene 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Only 15% of 3aa obtained, indicating covalently pre-installed quinoline directing group is essential

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, Co(acac)2, NaOPiv mixtures
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peaks at 1.11 V and 1.61 V appear only with 1a+Co+NaOPiv, supporting formation/oxidation of a Co(III)-amide intermediate rather than direct substrate oxidation (1a alone at 1.66 V; 2a at 1.94 V)

notes: Authors derivatize product 3aa (Pd/C-H2 to 4; LiAlH4 to 5) to demonstrate synthetic utility. The covalently bound 8-aminoquinoline directing group is mechanistically essential — a stepwise three-component variant fails (15%). No quenching of CV signal by alkene 2a argues against alkene oxidation as a productive step.
